subject: chore(audit): hygiene pass + document element-selector exceptions
body:
#10 — SettingsPane.jsx broken indentation
  Lines 105-216 had inconsistent indentation after the drawer
  migration: body div opened at indent 16 but children sat at indent
  20-28 (off by 4). The Work Hours hint div sat at indent 20 (sibling
  of mb-6 visually) when it should have been a child at indent 24.
  React parsed it fine, but the indentation misled readers about the
  tree structure. Re-indented the entire body block consistently.
  Pure cosmetic, no behavior change.

#11 — Dead --z-drawer / --z-drawer-backdrop CSS vars
  The :root z-index scale had `--z-drawer-backdrop: 28` and
  `--z-drawer: 30` left over from the pre-DaisyUI-drawer
  `.filter-sidebar` / `.detail-pane` / `.settings-pane` custom CSS.
  Those classes are gone; nothing references the vars anymore.
  Deleted both lines + updated the comment block.

#14 — Stale 30-line heatmap migration comment in styles.css
  The lines 276-308 comment described the `.heatmap-grid` /
  `.heatmap-header` / `.heatmap-label` / `.heatmap-cell` / `.heatmap-cell-sm`
  / `.heatmap-tooltip-inner` migration with arbitrary-bracket values
  (`text-[9px] sm:text-[11px]`, `grid-cols-[36px_repeat(7,1fr)]`,
  `shadow-[0_2px_8px_rgba(0,0,0,0.3)]`). All those arbitrary values
  were also deleted in Phase 3 — the comment described a transitional
  state that no longer exists. Replaced with a 7-line summary
  pointing to Timing.jsx for the current implementation.

#8 + #9 — Element-selector exceptions documented in code + CLAUDE.md
  styles.css has 4 element-selector / pseudo-element rules that
  technically violate "vanilla only, no custom CSS":
    - `* { font-family: var(--font-sans) }` global font reset
    - `body { background-color, color }` DaisyUI fallbacks
    - `h1, h2, h3 { font-family: var(--font-mono) }` mono headings
    - `body::before` decorative grid background

  These were deemed unavoidable:
    - Pseudo-elements cannot be Tailwind utilities
    - The font-family reset / heading styling is app-wide design
      intent applied via semantic HTML rather than per-consumer
      utility classes (~15-20 JSX edits to migrate)
    - The grid background is the dashboard's visual signature

  Added a 30-line block comment in styles.css explaining the four
  exceptions with rationale and rejected alternatives. Also added a
  reference in CLAUDE.md "Frontend: Styles and Scripts" so future
  sessions know these element-selector rules are sanctioned.

Verified: vite build clean (2.82s), 60/60 tests pass.

Tags: chore, hygiene, docs, daisyui, vanilla
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-v5-migration
Semver: patch
title: hygiene pass + document element-selector exceptions
